Messages

Channels

Servers

Direct Messages

Voice

API Keys

WebSocket

On this page

Messages ​

Send a Message ​

POST/api/channels/{channelId}/messages

Sends a message to a text channel. The authenticated user (or bot) must be a member of the server that owns the channel.

Request body

json
{
  "content": "Hello, world!",
  "nonce": "optional-client-dedup-id",
  "attachment_url": "https://cdn.example.com/file.png",
  "attachment_name": "file.png",
  "attachment_type": "image/png"
}
FieldTypeRequiredDescription
contentstring✅ (if no attachment)Message text
noncestring—Client-generated dedup ID; echoed back in the response
attachment_urlstring✅ (if no content)URL of an attached file
attachment_namestring—Display filename for the attachment
attachment_typestring—MIME type of the attachment

Response 201 Created

json
{
  "id": "123456789",
  "channel_id": "987654321",
  "author_id": "111",
  "author": "dylan",
  "content": "Hello, world!",
  "created_at": "2026-03-14T12:34:56Z",
  "nonce": "optional-client-dedup-id",
  "author_is_bot": false,
  "via_api": true
}
Bot vs. User API Key differences

via_api is true for both bot and user API keys — it just means the message came through the API rather than the web client.

Bot Key — message is from the bot user:

json
{ "author": "MyBot", "author_is_bot": true, "via_api": true }

Shows a purple BOT badge in chat (author_is_bot takes precedence).

User Key (Selfbot) — message is from you:

json
{ "author": "dylan", "author_is_bot": false, "via_api": true }

Shows a 🤖 selfbot icon in chat.

Get Messages ​

GET/api/channels/{channelId}/messages?limit=50&offset=0

Returns messages in a channel, newest first.

Query parameters

ParameterDefaultDescription
limit50Number of messages (max 100)
offset0Pagination offset

Response 200 OK

json
[
  {
    "id": "123456789",
    "channel_id": "987654321",
    "author_id": "111",
    "author": "dylan",
    "content": "Hello!",
    "created_at": "2026-03-14T12:34:56Z",
    "reactions": [],
    "author_is_bot": false,
    "via_api": false
  }
]

Edit a Message ​

PUT/api/messages/{messageId}

Edits the content of a message. Only the original author can edit.

Request body

json
{ "content": "Edited content" }

Response 200 OK — returns the updated message object.

Delete a Message ​

DELETE/api/messages/{messageId}

Deletes a message. Only the original author (or a server admin) can delete.

Response 204 No Content

Toggle Reaction ​

POST/api/messages/{messageId}/reactions

Adds or removes an emoji reaction. If you already reacted with this emoji, the reaction is removed.

Request body

json
{ "emoji": "👍" }

Response 204 No Content

Message Object ​

ts
interface Message {
  id: string
  channel_id: string
  author_id: string
  author: string            // username
  avatar_url?: string
  content: string
  created_at: string        // ISO 8601
  updated_at?: string       // set on edits
  nonce?: string
  attachment_url?: string
  attachment_name?: string
  attachment_type?: string
  reactions: Reaction[]
  author_is_bot: boolean    // true if sent by a bot user
  via_api: boolean          // true if sent via a User API Key (selfbot)
}

interface Reaction {
  emoji: string
  count: number
  user_ids: string[]
}
